Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Active learning framework to optimize process parameters for additive-manufactured Ti-6Al-4V with high strength and ductility

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition of Ti-6Al-4V (Ti-6Al-4V) gas-atomized metallic powder used for LPBF fabrication

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: LPBF using Concept Laser M2 (GE Additive, USA); layer thickness 50 um; rotation angle 67 deg; hatch distance 48 um; laser power 250 W; scan speed 1100 mm/s; volumetric energy density 94.69 J/mm3; post-heat treatment at 900 degC for 2 h; furnace cooling under argon atmosphere
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF with laser power 250 W, scan speed 1100 mm/s, VED 94.69 J/mm3, followed by heat treatment at 900 degC for 2 h with furnace cooling

Process_Info.Equipment: Concept Laser M2, GE Additive, USA

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully acicular microstructure with Widmanstätten structure comprising elongated alpha laths; alpha lath thickness ~2.62 um (thickest among samples); prior-beta grain width influenced by processing parameters; PF in (0001)alpha direction parallel to building direction; higher VED resulted in slower cooling and more alpha lath coarsening
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Acicular Widmanstätten alpha microstructure with coarse alpha laths (~2.62 um); dual-phase alpha+beta structure with strong basal texture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Strain rate 5e-3 s-1; digital image correlation (ARAMIS 12M)
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Dog-bone specimen, gauge length 5 mm, gauge width 2.5 mm, thickness 1.0 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Three specimens tested; average value reported

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Strain rate 5e-3 s-1; digital image correlation (ARAMIS 12M)
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Dog-bone specimen, gauge length 5 mm, gauge width 2.5 mm, thickness 1.0 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Three specimens tested; average value reported; highest TE (18.3%) achieved among all samples

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition of Ti-6Al-4V (Ti-6Al-4V) gas-atomized metallic powder used for LPBF fabrication

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully acicular microstructure with Widmanstätten structure; alpha lath thickness ~0.71 um (thinnest among all samples); highest UTS among all samples; prior-beta grain width smallest; PF in (0001)alpha direction shows ordered increase in intensity compared to other low-temperature HT samples; signs of localized softening leading to reduced ductility
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine acicular Widmanstätten alpha microstructure with finest alpha laths (~0.71 um); strong fiber texture; shows localized softening

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: LPBF using Concept Laser M2 (GE Additive, USA); layer thickness 50 um; rotation angle 67 deg; hatch distance 48 um; laser power 200 W; scan speed 1850 mm/s; volumetric energy density 45.04 J/mm3; post-heat treatment at 595 degC for 2 h; furnace cooling under argon atmosphere; identified as optimal combination for balanced high UTS and TE
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF with laser power 200 W, scan speed 1850 mm/s, VED 45.04 J/mm3, followed by low-temperature heat treatment at 595 degC for 2 h with furnace cooling; optimal balanced properties

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully acicular Widmanstätten microstructure; alpha lath thickness ~0.97 um (slightly higher than 2-1); prior-beta grain width influences alpha lath formation; PF in (0001)alpha shows highest intensity among low-temperature HT samples indicating strongest fiber texture; SF frequency 36.80% between 0.4-0.5; intermediate ductility between 1-1 and 2-1
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine acicular Widmanstätten alpha microstructure with alpha laths ~0.97 um; strong basal texture; balanced strength-ductility microstructure

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: LPBF using Concept Laser M2 (GE Additive, USA); layer thickness 50 um; rotation angle 67 deg; hatch distance 48 um; laser power 200 W; scan speed 1900 mm/s; volumetric energy density 43.85 J/mm3; post-heat treatment at 595 degC for 2 h; furnace cooling under argon atmosphere; achieved ultimate UTS 1190 MPa and TE 16.5%
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF with laser power 200 W, scan speed 1900 mm/s, VED 43.85 J/mm3, followed by low-temperature heat treatment at 595 degC for 2 h with furnace cooling; achieves high balanced properties

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully acicular Widmanstätten microstructure; alpha lath thickness ~0.90 um (similar to 3-1); prior-beta grain width influences microstructure; PF in (0001)alpha shows intermediate intensity between 2-1 and 3-1; SF frequency 46.38% between 0.4-0.5 indicating better ductility than 2-1; higher SF than 3-1 suggests easier slip activation
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine acicular Widmanstätten alpha microstructure with alpha laths ~0.90 um; intermediate texture; favorable slip activation for ductility
